Ristorante.title: Ristorante
Ristorante.description: Represents a restaurant registered in the PLACEAT system, including its profile, account status, subscription details, and core operational configurations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Ristorante entity.

nome.description: The official name of the restaurant.

tipo.description: The type of cuisine or establishment.

indirizzo.description: The physical address of the restaurant.

latitude.description: The geographical latitude coordinate of the restaurant's location.

longitude.description: The geographical longitude coordinate of the restaurant's location.

telefono.description: The primary contact phone number for the restaurant.

email.description: The primary contact email address for the restaurant.

proprietarioUid.description: Reference to the owner user's UID from the external authentication system. (Relationship: ExternalUser 1:N Ristorante)

stato.description: The current account status of the restaurant (e.g., trial, active, past_due, cancelled).

piano.description: The current subscription plan for the restaurant (e.g., free, pro, multi).

trialEndsAt.description: The date and time when the trial period for the restaurant's account ends.

stripeCustomerId.description: Identifier for the customer in Stripe's billing system.

stripeSubscriptionId.description: Identifier for the active subscription in Stripe's billing system.

durataTurnoDefault.description: Default duration in minutes for a dining shift or table turnover.

fusoOrario.description: The IANA time zone identifier for the restaurant (e.g., 'Europe/Rome').

reviewFlowConfigurationId.description: Reference to the ReviewFlowConfiguration entity for this restaurant. (Relationship: Ristorante 1:1 ReviewFlowConfiguration)

onboardingCompletato.description: Indicates if the restaurant's onboarding wizard has been fully completed.

onboardingStep.description: The current step number in the onboarding wizard (0-4).

createdAt.description: The date and time when the restaurant entity was created.

ReviewFlowConfiguration.title: ReviewFlowConfiguration
ReviewFlowConfiguration.description: Stores the settings for a restaurant's automated customer review request system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ReviewFlowConfiguration entity. This typically matches the Ristorante ID for a 1:1 relationship.

ristoranteId.description: Reference to the Ristorante entity this configuration belongs to. (Relationship: Ristorante 1:1 ReviewFlowConfiguration)

attivo.description: Indicates if the review flow automation is currently active.

googleLink.description: The direct link to the restaurant's Google My Business review page.

timingInvio.description: The number of hours after a reservation is completed before a review request is sent.

qrCodeUrl.description: The URL of the generated QR code for direct review submission.

DailyOpeningHours.title: DailyOpeningHours
DailyOpeningHours.description: Defines a single opening and closing time slot for a specific day of the week for a restaurant.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DailyOpeningHours entity.

ristoranteId.description: Reference to the Ristorante entity this opening slot belongs to. (Relationship: Ristorante 1:N DailyOpeningHours)

dayOfWeek.description: The day of the week for this opening slot (e.g., 'lunedi', 'martedi').

aperto.description: Indicates if the restaurant is considered 'open' during this specific time slot.

dalle.description: The start time of the opening slot (e.g., 'HH:MM').

alle.description: The end time of the opening slot (e.g., 'HH:MM').

slotIndex.description: An index to order multiple opening slots for the same day (e.g., '1' for morning, '2' for evening).

Tavolo.title: Tavolo
Tavolo.description: Represents a physical table within a restaurant's floor plan, including its layout coordinates, dimensions, capacity, and status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Tavolo entity.

ristoranteId.description: Reference to the Ristorante entity this table belongs to. (Relationship: Ristorante 1:N Tavolo)

numero.description: The visible number or identifier for the table within the restaurant.

tipo.description: The shape type of the table (e.g., 'rotondo', 'rettangolare').

x.description: The X-coordinate of the table's position on the floor plan canvas.

y.description: The Y-coordinate of the table's position on the floor plan canvas.

width.description: The width of the table in canvas units.

height.description: The height of the table in canvas units.

rotation.description: The rotation angle of the table in degrees (0-360).

raggio.description: The radius of the table in canvas units, applicable only for 'rotondo' tables.

capienza.description: The maximum number of people (capacity) the table can seat.

zona.description: The designated zone or area where the table is located (e.g., 'sala', 'dehor').

attivo.description: Indicates if the table is currently active and available for bookings.

Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a customer who has made a reservation at a restaurant, capturing basic contact information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Customer entity.

ristoranteId.description: Reference to the Ristorante entity where this customer made a booking. (Relationship: Ristorante 1:N Customer)

nome.description: The full name of the customer.

telefono.description: The contact phone number of the customer.

email.description: The contact email address of the customer.

Prenotazione.title: Prenotazione
Prenotazione.description: Represents a specific customer reservation for a table at a restaurant, including all relevant booking details and status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Prenotazione entity.

ristoranteId.description: Reference to the Ristorante entity where this reservation was made. (Relationship: Ristorante 1:N Prenotazione)

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer entity who made the reservation. (Relationship: Customer 1:N Prenotazione)

tavoloId.description: Reference to the Tavolo entity assigned for this reservation. (Relationship: Tavolo 1:N Prenotazione)

numeroPersone.description: The total number of people for this reservation.

stato.description: The current status of the reservation (e.g., 'confermata', 'completata', 'cancellata', 'no-show').

note.description: Additional notes or special requests for the reservation.

recensioneInviata.description: Indicates if a review request has already been sent for this reservation.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the reservation was created.

Muro.title: Muro
Muro.description: Defines a segment of a wall in a restaurant's interactive floor plan.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Muro entity.

ristoranteId.description: Reference to the Ristorante entity this wall segment belongs to. (Relationship: Ristorante 1:N Muro)

pointsX.description: An ordered list of X-coordinates for the points defining the wall segment.

pointsY.description: An ordered list of Y-coordinates for the points defining the wall segment, corresponding to pointsX.

spessore.description: The thickness of the wall segment in canvas units.

Zona.title: Zona
Zona.description: Defines a distinct, colored area (zone) within a restaurant's floor plan, used for logical grouping or visual segmentation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Zona entity.

ristoranteId.description: Reference to the Ristorante entity this zone belongs to. (Relationship: Ristorante 1:N Zona)

colore.description: The hexadecimal color code (with opacity) used to visually represent the zone.

pathX.description: An ordered list of X-coordinates for the points defining the polygon path of the zone.

pathY.description: An ordered list of Y-coordinates for the points defining the polygon path of the zone, corresponding to pathX.

definition.description: Top-level collection for all restaurant profiles. Each document represents a unique restaurant and includes core information, account status, and subscription details. Ownership is defined by 'proprietarioUid'.

Subcollection storing the single ReviewFlowConfiguration document for a specific restaurant. The document ID is 'config' for a 1:1 relationship. Includes denormalized 'proprietarioUid' and 'ristoranteIsActive' boolean for authorization independence.

Subcollection for daily opening hours configuration for a specific restaurant. Allows multiple time slots per day. Includes denormalized 'proprietarioUid' and 'ristoranteIsActive' boolean for authorization independence.

params.name: dailyOpeningHoursId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific opening hour slot.

definition.description: Subcollection defining individual tables within a restaurant's floor plan. Includes denormalized 'proprietarioUid' and 'ristoranteIsActive' boolean for authorization independence.
Subcollection storing customer profiles associated with a specific restaurant. Includes denormalized 'proprietarioUid' and 'ristoranteIsActive' boolean for authorization independence.
Subcollection for customer reservations made at a specific restaurant. Includes denormalized 'proprietarioUid' and 'ristoranteIsActive' boolean for authorization independence.
Subcollection for wall segments in a restaurant's interactive floor plan. Includes denormalized 'proprietarioUid' and 'ristoranteIsActive' boolean for authorization independence.

params.name: muroId
params.description: Unique identifier for the wall segment.

definition.description: Subcollection for defining distinct colored zones within a restaurant's floor plan. Includes denormalized 'proprietarioUid' and 'ristoranteIsActive' boolean for authorization independence.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for PLACEAT is designed to prioritize Authorization Independence, Debuggability, and Query-Ability for secure and scalable operations. The core principle applied is **Authorization Independence via Denormalization** (Mandate A), which dictates that security-critical attributes from parent documents (like ownership and active status) must be copied into all relevant subcollection documents. This completely eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls in security rules, which break atomic operations and hinder debuggability.

The chosen structure adopts a hierarchical model starting with a top-level `ristoranti` collection, where each document represents a unique restaurant. All related entities (tables, reservations, opening hours, customers, floor plan elements, and review configurations) are stored as subcollections under their respective `ristoranteId`. This leverages **Hierarchical Paths for User-Owned Data** (Mandate C.2), ensuring that data is inherently scoped to a specific restaurant and, by extension, its owner.

To achieve **Authorization Independence** and support **Query-Able Security Rules (QAPs)**:
1.  **`proprietarioUid` Denormalization:** The `proprietarioUid` (owner's Firebase UID) from the parent `Ristorante` document is denormalized and stored in *every document* within its subcollections (`reviewFlowConfiguration`, `dailyOpeningHours`, `tavoli`, `customers`, `prenotazioni`, `muri`, `zone`). This allows security rules for subcollections to directly verify ownership by comparing `request.auth.uid` with `resource.data.proprietarioUid` without needing to fetch the parent `Ristorante` document.
2.  **`ristoranteIsActive` Denormalization:** An `ristoranteIsActive` boolean field is also denormalized into all subcollection documents. This field, derived from the `Ristorante`'s `stato` and `trialEndsAt` status, provides an atomic and easily verifiable status for determining if the restaurant's subscription is active. A Cloud Function would be responsible for updating this field across all relevant subcollection documents whenever the parent `Ristorante`'s status or trial end date changes.

This denormalization strategy significantly simplifies Firestore security rules, making them highly performant (no cross-document reads), atomic (rules can evaluate based on a single document's state), and extremely debuggable. For example, a rule for a reservation document would simply check `resource.data.proprietarioUid == request.auth.uid && resource.data.ristoranteIsActive == true && resource.data.ristoranteId == ristoranteId` (where `ristoranteId` is from the path wildcard). This directly enables **QAPs** because client-side `list` queries can include `where('proprietarioUid', '==', request.auth.uid)` and `where('ristoranteId', '==', specificRistoranteId)`, ensuring only relevant and authorized data is returned.

**Structural Segregation** (Mandate B) is maintained as each subcollection (`tavoli`, `prenotazioni`, etc.) stores documents of a single entity type with homogeneous security requirements. There is no mixing of public and private data within the same collection.

**Access Modeling** (Mandate C) uses the path-based ownership for restaurant-specific data and continues this hierarchy for all nested entities. **DBAC** (Mandate C.4) is achieved by storing `proprietarioUid` directly in the database, with no reliance on custom claims.

**Data Clarity and Predictability** (Mandate D) is addressed by using explicit state fields like `Ristorante.stato` and `Prenotazione.stato`, and ensuring consistent, semantic naming conventions (e.g., `ristoranteId`, `proprietarioUid`).

Overall, this design ensures a secure, scalable, and easily maintainable Firestore architecture for PLACEAT, adhering to all critical design principles and mandates.
